title: I almost didn't survive the 2024 freight crash. Here's what kept BCAT alive.
modeledOn: Codie Sanchez / Colby Baskin — contrarian retelling of risk and reward with Midwestern dry humor
whyItWorks: Personal near-crisis + macro freight-market read. Hits the 2026 market-cycle narrative while anchoring to BCAT history. Naming the survival lever (agent-first quoting, not luck) builds authority for JobsDone Labs.

linkedin.hook: I almost didn't survive the 2024 freight crash. Here's what kept BCAT alive.
linkedin.description: Open with near-exit context, 3 hard lessons about the freight cycle and operator's advantage, pivot to building JobsDone Labs as the next evolution. Vulnerability over polish.
linkedin.caption:
I almost didn't survive the 2024 freight crash.

It was early 2024, margins were thin enough to make your eyes water, and I seriously thought walking away was the smart play.

I didn't. Not because I'm tough. Because I realized I was treating the symptom — rate volatility — instead of the cause: operational speed.

If we could outrun the rate swings with smarter quoting, faster carrier outreach, and tighter follow-up, the cycle was secondary.

So we built. Agent-first integrations. Lead scoring. Auto-follow-up sequences.

Same headcount. 2.3x more accepted shipments.

The lesson: adversity is usually telling you where to invest, not when to exit.

2026 is the year agent-first logistics becomes table stakes — and I'm not selling. I'm building it.

DM me 'BCAT' if you're building freight ops for the next market cycle.
linkedin.aiCreativePrompt: Montage: BCAT office at night with warm lamp light, then green shipping-label stamp on cream paper, then agent dashboard with rising curve on #0B0D12 screen. Cream #F4EFE3 text with #1466FF accent grain over top.
scriptSparknotes: Opener: 'I almost didn't survive the 2024 freight crash.'
Context: thin margins and macro downward.
Action: built agent stack instead of selling.
Result: 2.3x more accepted shipments, same headcount.
Meta lesson: adversity is an investment signal.

tiktok.hook: I almost sold my freight company in 2024.
tiktok.description: Condensed near-exit story with agent-stack reveal. Text overlays emphasize the pivot.
tiktok.caption:
I almost sold my freight company in 2024.

Margins were brutal. I was done.
Then I realized: the market doesn't kill bad luck.
It kills slow operators.

Same headcount. 2.3x more shipments.

We built the agent stack instead of selling.

DM 'BCAT' if you're building for the next cycle.
